title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur from July 11-16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries for the US 250th anniversary.
Previous Sail Boston events took place in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, attracting millions of spectators.
Past parades faced protests due to the ships' historical association with the slave trade, stirring controversy during the 1992 and 2000 events.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returned to Patriots rookie minicamp, improving the team's vibe after an offseason scandal.
Vrabel's energetic presence is noted as similar to last year, easing concerns about off-field issues affecting performance.
New safety Kevin Byard vouches for Vrabel's consistent coaching style, as the Patriots prepare for a challenging season.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run homer in the eighth inning, lifting the Red Sox to a 3-2 win over the Braves.
Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle threw a career-high eight innings, allowing two runs and four hits with three strikeouts.
Closer Aroldis Chapman secured the save in the ninth, despite facing a bases-loaded situation, finalizing the Red Sox's comeback victory.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Red Sox placed Trevor Story on the 10-day injured list with a sports hernia.
Nick Sogard was recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Trevor Story on the roster.
Story's possible hernia surgery could lead to a 6-10 week recovery period.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died in a head-on crash with a school bus in Marlborough, New Hampshire, Friday morning.
The collision involved a pickup truck and a school bus carrying 13 students; none of the students were seriously injured.
The crash is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police and other local authorities for further details.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Atlanta Braves' Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning against the Boston Red Sox, winning 3-2.
Red Sox starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs over five innings, striking out six batters.
Boston's offense struggled, leaving seven men on base and batting 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots and Stefon Diggs reportedly have mutual interest in a reunion, but it remains unlikely due to trade obstacles.
A potential trade for A.J. Brown is a significant roadblock to the Patriots re-signing wide receiver Stefon Diggs.
New England faces financial constraints with A.J. Brown's $32 million salary and Romeo Doubs' $68 million contract commitments.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police are searching for Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, a 15-year-old missing from Dorchester since Tuesday.
LaGrant-Gomes, described as Black, 5'8", with brown eyes, was last seen on Blue Hill Avenue.
Authorities urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or use CrimeStoppers for anonymous tips.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, is accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on Mother's Day in Dorchester.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder and unlawful possession of a firearm.
Both victims, aged 42, sustained injuries; the mother has life-threatening injuries while the man's injuries are non-life-threatening.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA in the 2026 MLB season.
A lineup of former Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, Kyle Schwarber, and Rafael Devers.
Dominic Smith, a former Red Sox player, is batting over .350 as of May 15, 2026.
